en: Alright Benjamin, playtime's over. What the hell did you do to my computer?
ko: 좋아 벤자민, 장난은 끝이야. 내 컴퓨터에 도대체 뭘 한 거야?

en: I didn't do anything to it other than increase its performance.
ko: 성능 올린 거 말고는 아무것도 안 했어요.

en: If that's your idea of increasing performance, then you ought to rethink your career.
ko: 그게 성능 향상이라면 직업을 다시 생각해봐.

en: Because that thing has crashed more times than Mel Gibson on his way home from a Saturday night.
ko: 토요일 밤 귀가하는 멜 깁슨보다 더 많이 다운됐거든.

en: Wait a second, are you smiling? Of course not. You're yelling at me. Why would I be smiling?
ko: 잠깐, 웃고 있어? 당연히 아니죠. 저한테 소리 지르는데 왜 웃겠어요?

en: Don't bullshit a bullshitter, Benjamin. You're up to something and you better come clean right now.
ko: 사기꾼한테 구라치지 마, 벤자민. 뭔가 꾸미고 있지? 당장 실토해.

en: Okay. I was hoping to do this in a more formal environment, but...
ko: 좋아요. 좀 더 격식 있는 자리에서 하려고 했는데...

en: Allow me to introduce the Donna. What the hell's that?
ko: '더 도나'를 소개할게요. 그게 뭔데?

en: It's a digital version of you.
ko: 당신의 디지털 버전이에요.

en: With approximately 10,000 of your best quotes, witticisms, and zingers packed into 5 gigahertz of computing power.
ko: 당신의 최고 명언, 재치, 한 마디 약 1만 개를 5GHz 컴퓨팅 파워에 담았어요.

en: That's why you've been personally updating my computer and nobody else's?
ko: 그래서 내 컴퓨터만 직접 업데이트한 거야?

en: Yes. I've been secretly recording you for the better part of two years. Two years?
ko: 네. 거의 2년간 몰래 녹음했어요. 2년?

en: Why me? Because everyone always wishes they could say the right thing at the right time.
ko: 왜 나야? 누구나 적절한 때에 적절한 말을 하고 싶어하잖아요.

en: And you are the one woman in the universe who says the right thing every time.
ko: 그리고 당신은 매번 딱 맞는 말을 하는 유일한 여자예요.

en: Not to mention, you are without a doubt the most amazing legal secretary in the history of everything.
ko: 게다가 역사상 가장 대단한 법률 비서라는 것도 의심의 여지가 없고요.

en: Benjamin, that's the sweetest thing anyone's ever said to me.
ko: 벤자민, 지금까지 들은 중에 가장 달콤한 말이야.

en: Okay, but you said it about yourself two weeks ago. That I did.
ko: 근데 2주 전에 본인이 한 말이에요. 맞아, 내가 했지.

en: In that case, Donna Paulson, I wasn't going to ask you this for another few weeks,
ko: 그렇다면 도나 폴슨, 몇 주 후에 물어보려고 했는데,

en: but how would you feel about pursuing this with me as a business opportunity?
ko: 이걸 비즈니스 기회로 같이 추진해보는 거 어때요?

en: Let me think about it. But first, let me borrow that thing for a few days.
ko: 생각 좀 해볼게. 근데 먼저 그거 며칠만 빌려줘.

en: Jesus Christ. Harvey's handwriting is so bad, he makes my doctor look like a calligrapher.
ko: 세상에. 하비 글씨가 너무 못생겨서 우리 의사가 서예가로 보일 정도야.

en: You're telling me, sister. That handwriting's sloppier than Mel Gibson on a Saturday night.
ko: 내 말이, 언니. 토요일 밤 멜 깁슨보다 더 엉망이야.

en: Holy shit. You can adjust my phrases to the situation at hand?
ko: 대박. 상황에 맞게 내 말을 조절할 수 있어?

en: Of course I can. Who do you think you're dealing with?
ko: 당연하지. 누구 상대하는 건지 알아?

en: As a matter of fact, I just incorporated holy shit into my vocabulary right now.
ko: 사실 방금 '대박'을 내 어휘에 추가했어.

en: No way. That had to have already been in there. Well, look who finally showed up to the party.
ko: 말도 안 돼. 이미 있었을 텐데. 드디어 파티에 나타났네.

en: The party written by, produced by, and starring the Donna.
ko: '더 도나'가 기획, 제작, 주연한 파티에.

en: You should know that we get pitched six personal assistant devices a month.
ko: 저희는 한 달에 개인 비서 기기 제안을 6건씩 받습니다.

en: And you should know that our device makes Siri and the Alexa look like toaster ovens.
ko: 저희 기기는 시리와 알렉사를 토스터기처럼 보이게 만들어요.

en: Well, that may be the case, but I'm concerned that the market is already saturated.
ko: 그럴 수도 있지만 시장이 이미 포화 상태인 게 걱정이에요.

en: If you were so concerned, you wouldn't be sitting here in the first place. Is that a fact?
ko: 그렇게 걱정됐으면 애초에 여기 앉아 있지도 않았겠죠. 그게 사실인가요?

en: Yes, it is. Because you're the head of the investment team,
ko: 네. 당신이 투자팀 팀장이니까요.

en: and the head of the team doesn't waste his time unless he thinks there might be something in it for him.
ko: 팀장은 자기한테 이득이 있다고 생각하지 않으면 시간 낭비 안 하죠.

en: There might be other things in it for me than just investing.
ko: 투자 말고 다른 이득이 있을 수도 있죠.

en: Yes, there could. Like marking your territory.
ko: 그럴 수 있죠. 영역 표시 같은 거요.

en: Because anybody can see by the way you two are sitting there that he's gunning for your job, and you don't like that one bit.
ko: 둘이 앉은 꼴 보면 누구나 알아요. 저 사람이 당신 자리를 노리고 있고, 당신은 그게 싫은 거.

en: That's not— Who told you that? No one told me that.
ko: 그건 아니... 누가 그랬어요? 아무도 안 그랬어요.

en: Just like no one told me that you can't fire him because that'll make you look weak.
ko: 짜르면 약해 보이니까 못 짜른다는 것도 아무도 안 알려줬고요.

en: Which is why you took this meeting, because you didn't want him taking it without you.
ko: 그래서 이 미팅에 온 거예요. 그가 혼자 하는 게 싫으니까.

en: Okay. How in the hell are you doing all this? I'm doing this because I'm Donna.
ko: 좋아요. 도대체 어떻게 이러는 거야? 내가 도나니까.

en: And if it hasn't dawned on you already, so has this device.
ko: 아직 모르겠으면, 이 기기도 마찬가지예요.

en: Now, do you want to puff up your chest and play office politics,
ko: 이제 가슴 펴고 사내 정치를 할 건가요,

en: or do you want to hear about an opportunity that could vault you both to the top of the ladder?
ko: 아니면 둘 다 꼭대기로 올려줄 기회 얘기를 들을 건가요?

en: Why don't you tell us about your product?
ko: 제품 설명해주시죠.
